text: The surgeon encounters filmy adhesions attached to the right hepatic margin, indicating a delicate separation process is required.
The duodenum is visible, slightly protruding into the surgical field.
The cystic duct becomes visible in the background of the surgical field.
The surgeon establishes the critical view of safety, revealing the posterior branch of the cystic artery coming into view.
The surgeon visually confirms the identification of the cystic duct and cystic artery, clearly distinguishing these structures within the surgical field.
The surgeon exposes the gallbladder, showing fat tissue fusing it laterally to the right hepatic margin, with a visibly narrow cystic plate.
